reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly in multiple areas. First, regarding Content Accuracy, the model hallucinates and adds the phrase '快快快快快' (Quick, quick, quick, quick, quick) at the end of the audio (00:03-00:04), which was not in the script. Second, regarding the target feature 'fast speaking rate' (语速), the core sentence '我今天上午忙死了，处理了许多紧急邮件' (00:00-00:03) is spoken at a normal to slow pace, characterized by a dragging, complaining tone rather than a fast, rushed pace. It completely fails to meet the user's requirement for a fast speed.
reasoning_model_2: The model performs excellently. The Content Accuracy is perfect, matching the script exactly with no omissions or additions. Regarding the target feature 'fast speaking rate', the model delivers the entire sentence in approximately 2 seconds (00:00-00:02). The syllables are articulated rapidly and fluidly, effectively simulating a natural human rushing to speak. The rhythm and breathlessness associated with speaking quickly are well-represented, meeting the 'fully correct' standard.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 2 followed the speed constraint perfectly, generating a natural, fast-paced audio that matched the script exactly. Model 1 failed to generate a fast speaking rate (speaking at a normal/slow pace instead) and committed a severe content error by hallucinating extra words ('快快快快快') at the end of the clip. Model 1 failed on both content and style instructions.
